O Eyilcim et al
Resorcinol (1.0 mmol) and β-ketoester (1.0 mmol) were interacted with ultrasonic waves in an ultrasonic bath at
room temperature in 20 ml THF as solvent and catalyst. The progress of the reaction was followed by thin layer
chromatography with a dichloromethane: methanol (20:1) system. It was determined that the reaction was
completed in 5 h. THF was removed in a rotary evaporator. The resulting solid was crystallized from methanol.

Coumarin compound (7a-c) (1 mmol) dissolved in acetone (50 ml) was added superheated K2CO3 (3 mmol).
After reﬂuxing under nitrogen atmosphere for one hour, dihaloalkane (1,4-Dibromobutane, 1,6-
dibromohexane or 1,8-dibromooctane) (2 mmol) was added. The reaction mixture was reﬂuxed at 56 °C for
4–24 h and the reaction was monitored by TLC. The solution was brought to room temperature and the solvent
was removed by ﬁltering. It was crystallized from ethanol to obtain compounds 8a-e, respectively.
